issues.title: Missing filter for transaction status
issues.problem: The query should only include transactions with status 'COMPLETED' for calculating lifetime value.

issues.title: Incorrect aggregation
issues.line: SUM(t.amount) AS lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS total_orders
issues.problem: The query should only sum the amount for completed transactions, but it is not filtering for 'COMPLETED' status in the aggregation.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS lifetime_value

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, making it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.line: SUM(t.amount) AS lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS total_orders
issues.problem: The aliases 'lifetime_value' and 'total_orders' are not consistently named. Consider using 'total_lifetime_value' and 'total_transactions'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS total_transactions

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS total_transactions FROM dim_customer c LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY total_lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query has correctness issues with aggregation and missing status filter. Readability can be improved with comments and consistent naming.
